Product Title: Thioshield Thiamethoxam 12.6% + Lambda Cyhalothrin 9.5% ZC Insecticide for Sucking & Chewing Pest Control
Product Body:
Thioshield Thiamethoxam 12.6% + Lambda Cyhalothrin 9.5% ZC is a dual-action insecticide designed to control both sucking pests and chewing insects in crops.

Farmers often face pest problems like aphids, jassids, whiteflies, and caterpillars, especially during warm and humid conditions. These pests reduce crop growth and yield.

This insecticide helps manage these pests effectively and supports healthy plant growth and crop protection.

Nutrient Composition / Technical Composition
Active Ingredient: Thiamethoxam 12.6% + Lambda Cyhalothrin 9.5%
Formulation: ZC (Mixed formulation – suspension + capsule)
Type: Insecticide (Systemic + Contact action)
Category: Dual Mode Insecticide
 Key Benefits 
Controls sucking pests (aphids, jassids, whiteflies)
Effective against chewing pests and caterpillars
Provides quick knockdown and longer residual effect
Improves plant health by reducing pest damage
Helps maintain leaf quality and crop vigor
Supports better yield and crop quality
Suitable Crops
Suitable for multiple crops:

Cotton
Chilli
Tomato
Paddy (Rice)
Groundnut
Vegetables
Pulses
 Dosage and Application Method
Recommended Dosage
Application Type        Dose
Foliar Spray        100 – 120 ml per Acre
Per Liter Water        0.5 – 0.6 ml per Liter
Application Method
Mix recommended dose in 150–200 liters of water per acre
Spray uniformly on crop foliage
Apply at early stage of pest infestation

Tapride Cartap Hydrochloride 50% SP is a trusted and effective insecticide widely used by farmers to control stem borers, leaf folders, and other chewing pests, especially in paddy crops. Known in the market as a reliable alternative to Caldan 50 SP, Tapride insecticide helps farmers protect crops at the right time and secure better yields with confidence.

 Benefits of tapride insecticide 
Strong control of stem borers and chewing pests
Effective even at early larval stages
Fast action with long-lasting protection
Easy to dissolve and spray
Helps prevent major yield loss
Trusted by farmers for paddy cultivation
How the cartap 50 %sp Works
Cartap Hydrochloride works by blocking nerve signal transmission in insects. Once pests come in contact with or ingest the insecticide, they stop feeding immediately. This results in paralysis and death of pests, protecting crops from further damage. Tapride insecticide is especially effective against pests hidden inside plant stems.

Features
Active Ingredient: Cartap Hydrochloride 50% SP
Formulation: Soluble Powder (SP)
Strong stomach and contact action
Excellent penetration into plant tissues
Reliable performance in field conditions
 Dosage / Usage Instructions of TAPRIDE 50% sp 
Recommended Dosage

1 gram per liter of water
About 200–250 grams per acre, depending on crop and pest pressure
Application Method:

Dissolve the required quantity in a small amount of water
Add to spray tank and mix well
Spray evenly on crops, focusing on affected areas
Apply at the early stage of pest infestation for best results.

Suitable Crops
Paddy (Rice)
Sugarcane
Vegetables
Maize
Other crops affected by chewing pests
Why Choose Tapride Insecticide?
Trusted quality and formulation
Proven results similar to Caldan 50 SP
Reliable Cartap Hydrochloride 50 SP insecticide
Ideal for farmers searching for Tapride insecticide
Excellent value for money for paddy farmers

Product Title: Thiomax Thiamethoxam 25% WG – Trusted Systemic Insecticide for Sucking Pests
Product Body:
Thiomax (Thiamethoxam 25% WG) is a modern systemic insecticide designed to protect crops from harmful sucking insects. It gives fast action, long-lasting control, and strong plant protection from the inside. Easy to use and highly effective, Thiomax supports healthier crops and better harvest for farmers.

 Benefits of thiamethoxam 25 % wg 
Quickly controls major sucking pests
Systemic action protects the whole plant
Long-lasting effect with fewer sprays
Improves crop health and yield
Easy to mix and spray
Suitable for multiple crops
How Thiomax Works
Thiomax contains Thiamethoxam 25% WG, which is absorbed by the plant after spraying. The insecticide moves through the plant’s system. When pests feed on the plant sap, it affects their nervous system, stops feeding, and controls them effectively.

This deep, inside-plant action makes thiamethoxam insecticide more reliable than ordinary contact pesticides.

 Features of thiomax 25 
Active Ingredient: Thiamethoxam 25% WG
Formulation: Water dispersible granules
Mode of Action: Systemic
Fast knockdown and residual control
Rainfast after absorption
Trusted Thiomax quality
Dosage & Usage Instructions
Recommended Dosage:

40–80 grams per acre (depending on crop and pest level)

Application Method:

Mix required quantity in clean water
Spray uniformly on crop foliage
Cover both upper and lower leaf surfaces
Use only recommended dose and follow local agricultural advice.

Suitable Crops
Thiomax Thiamethoxam 25% WG is suitable for:

Cotton
Rice
Chilli
Vegetables
Pulses
Sugarcane
Target Pests
Aphids
Whiteflies
Jassids
Thrips
Leafhoppers
This makes it a reliable thiamethoxam pesticide for common pest problems.

Azoxystrobin 18.2% + Difenoconazole 11.4% SC is a broad-spectrum, systemic fungicide designed for preventive, curative, and eradicative control of various fungal diseases in multiple crops. This dual-action formulation combines:

Azoxystrobin (18.2%) – A strobilurin group fungicide that inhibits fungal respiration, preventing spore germination and disease development.

Difenoconazole (11.4%) – A triazole group fungicide that disrupts fungal sterol biosynthesis, stopping fungal growth and spread.

This highly effective combination ensures long-lasting disease control, enhanced plant health, and increased crop productivity.

Mode of Action:
Azoxystrobin 18.2% + Difenoconazole 11.4% SC works through:

1. Systemic Action: Quickly absorbed by leaves and stems, moving within plant tissues for internal protection.
2. Preventive, Curative & Eradicative Effect: Stops spore germination, inhibits fungal growth, and eliminates existing infections.

Dual-Mode Functionality:
Azoxystrobin disrupts mitochondrial respiration, stopping energy production in fungal cells.

Difenoconazole blocks ergosterol biosynthesis, weakening fungal cell membranes and stopping further growth.
Translaminar Activity: Moves across leaf surfaces, ensuring deep penetration and extended protection.
Rainfast & Long-Lasting Effect: Quickly absorbed, reducing wash-off risk and providing 15-20 days of protection.

Target Diseases Controlled:
This fungicide is effective against a wide range of fungal diseases, including:

1.Powdery Mildew (Erysiphe spp., Oidium spp.) – Grapes, Chilli, Cucurbits
2. Early & Late Blight (Alternaria solani, Phytophthora infestans) – Tomato, Potato
3. Sheath Blight (Rhizoctonia solani) – Rice
4. Rusts (Puccinia spp.) – Wheat, Soybean, Pulses
5. Anthracnose (Colletotrichum spp.) – Chilli, Mango
6. Leaf Spots (Cercospora spp., Septoria spp.) – Groundnut, Cotton
7. Downy Mildew (Plasmopara viticola) – Grapes
8. Fruit Rot & Scab (Venturia inaequalis, Colletotrichum spp.) – Apple, Citrus
9. Sigatoka Disease (Mycosphaerella spp.) – Banana

Recommended Crops:
Field Crops – Rice, Wheat, Maize, Soybean, Pulses
Vegetables – Tomato, Potato, Chilli, Cucurbits, Onion
Fruits – Grapes, Mango, Apple, Citrus, Banana
Oilseeds & Plantation Crops – Groundnut, Coffee, Tea
Ornamental & Turf Crops – Roses, Marigold, Lawns

Application and Dosage:
Azoxystrobin 18.2% + Difenoconazole 11.4% SC is applied as a foliar spray for effective disease prevention and control.

Humic Acid

Application : (How to Use Humic Acid for Plants)
Foliar Spray
1. Use 4–6 g per litre of water

2. Apply 800 g per acre per round

Soil Application

2. For best results, apply at early growth stages and repeat as per crop requirement.
